disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Burning epigastric discomfort with postprandial fullness for 4 weeks, acutely worse over 24 hours after a large meal
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me more about the discomfort after meals?
When did this start and how has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a burning, heavy feeling high in my tummy that comes on after I eat. It's been there a few weeks but it flared up badly after a big dinner last night.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Early satiation, upper-abdominal bloating and frequent belching, worst 30-60 minutes after eating
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel full quickly when you eat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I get full really fast and can't finish my meals, and I'm bloated and burping a lot, mostly about half an hour to an hour after eating.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No alarm features — denies haematemesis, coffee-ground vomiting, melena, dysphagia and unintentional weight loss (pertinent negatives)
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited any blood or noticed black tarry stools?
Any trouble swallowing or weight loss you didn't intend?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that. No blood coming up, no black or tarry poo, no problem swallowing, and I haven't lost any weight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No cardiac-sounding features — pain is strictly food-related and reproducible, not exertional, no radiation to arm or jaw, no sweating or breathlessness
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain come on with activity or exertion?
Does it spread to your arm, neck or jaw, or come with sweating or breathlessness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it only comes on after I eat, not when I'm walking or active. It doesn't spread anywhere and I'm not sweaty or short of breath with it.

trigger_question_examples: Do you feel sick or nauseated?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit queasy now and then, but I haven't actually thrown up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No regular prescription medications; no regular NSAID use; only intermittent over-the-counter antacids with negligible relief
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any regular medications?
Do you use anti-inflammatory painkillers like ibuprofen or naproxen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't take anything regularly. I don't use those anti-inflammatory painkillers. I've just tried some antacid tablets and they barely help.
